name: @forwardimpact/landmark
description: Surface engineering progress from activity evidence — outcomes visible without singling out individuals.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: The quarterly review is due and the only data available is ticket counts — which single out individuals rather than illuminate the system.
jobs.bigHire: demonstrate engineering progress without making individuals feel surveilled.
jobs.littleHire: tell whether culture investments are working before the next budget cycle.
jobs.competesWith: sprint velocity; ticket counts; annual reviews; asking managers 'how's the team doing?'; not measuring at all

forces.push: Pressure to show ROI with no way to separate signal from noise.
forces.pull: System-level trends that show direction without naming individuals.
forces.habit: Equating velocity and throughput with team health.
forces.anxiety: Measurement feels like surveillance regardless of intent.

jobs.firedWhen: metrics get used punitively; or leadership turnover deprioritizes measurement.

jobs.trigger: The promotion conversation ended with 'not yet' but no specifics — unclear what evidence would change the answer.
jobs.bigHire: show concrete evidence of growth, not just my manager's impression.
jobs.littleHire: check whether recent work shows visible progress toward the next level.
jobs.competesWith: annual review feedback; mentor conversations; LinkedIn Learning; hoping the next project makes readiness obvious; not pursuing promotion at all

forces.push: Career conversations feel subjective with no shared evidence base.
forces.pull: A clear picture of what's needed to close the gap, grounded in evidence.
forces.habit: Waiting for annual feedback rather than continuously self-assessing.
forces.anxiety: Structured analysis might confirm being further behind than assumed.

jobs.firedWhen: gap analysis recommends areas disconnected from actual promotion decisions; or a reorg changes what gets promoted, making the standard feel like last regime's artifact.
